summary: Strong first set with Black Throated Wind; second set uneven
blurb: The opening half of the night delivers high‑energy jams on Touch of Grey, Lazy River Road and a fiery Black Throated Wind. Jerry shines on Music > Deal and Deal, while the second set loses momentum despite a solid I Fought The Law opener. Overall a mixed show with standout moments worth hearing.
review: April 5, 1993 at Nassau Coliseum captures the Dead in a transitional mood. The first set rolls out with the newly‑minted Touch of Grey and a tight groove on Lazy River Road before exploding into a scorching Black Throated Wind that has Jerry’s lead guitar “on fire.” The Music Never Stopped segues straight into Deal, giving Garcia another chance to stretch his soloing muscles – reviewers called this “great Jerry solos” and highlighted the crisp interplay with Phil’s bass. By contrast, the second set starts strong with I Fought The Law but quickly sags; vocals sound canned and Bob’s energy wanes, while a series of jams (Way To Go Home → Corinna → He’s Gone → Drums → Space → I Need A Miracle) feels uneven to many listeners. Still, highlights like Wharf Rat and an enthusiastic Gloria encore keep the night from sinking entirely. For fans who want to hear the good moments without the tape‑hiss of some SBD copies, the SonicSolutions flac capture (gd1993-04-05.107784.fob.SonicSolutionsDSM-6P.flac16) offers clear sound and preserves the set’s high points.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive (touch of Grey enthusiasm and solid SonicSolutions capture) while the other two are mixed, resulting in an overall modestly favorable sentiment. Positive language such as “great Jerry solos,” “energetic,” and “standout” appears across multiple sources, outweighing the occasional criticism of flat vocals and uneven second set.

best_recording.reason: Good sound quality and clear capture of the band’s energy; includes praised performances of Lazy River Road, Deal and Wharf Rat.

key_highlights: First set delivers high‑energy jams with Black Throated Wind and tight grooves.
Jerry’s guitar shines on Music > Deal and Deal, delivering memorable solos.
I Fought The Law opens a vigorous second set before momentum drops.
Rare encore closing with Gloria/U.S. Blues adds an extra treat.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers standout lead work on Music > Deal, Black Throated Wind and Deal, though his playing tapers off in the later second‑set numbers.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides solid bass foundations; his lyric‑altered Tom Thumb line is praised, though some feel his song choices slightly disrupt set flow.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s vocals are described as rough and low‑energy, especially on the second‑set material.
band_performance.Vince: Keyboard work (likely Vince) earns kudos for piano solos that add texture during the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming blends into the extended Space/Drums segment; no specific criticism noted.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming supports the rhythmic drive, particularly in the high‑energy first set.
